# Think College Level

Cambridge (HSPS) Offer Holder | President of Kiwanis Voice Club of Nippon | CEO of ElevateOS | Lumiere Scholar

Howard Chan's profile, education, awards, projects, research, activities, and contact links.

## Pages
- Home: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/
- About: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/about/
- Awards: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/awards/
- Publications: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/
- Projects: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/portfolio/
- Contact: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/contact/

## About
- I am Chak Hang (Howard) Chan, a Tokyo-based builder and incoming HSPS offer holder at Peterhouse, University of Cambridge. I care about the point where politics, international systems, AI, service, and entrepreneurship meet real-world execution. Across the work I do, the common thread is coordination. I like problems where a better system makes the work easier to repeat, easier to hand off, and more useful in practice. That is why I keep building across service leadership, research, and product systems.

## Experience
- Founder and President, Kiwanis Voice Club of Nippon: Founded the first Voice Club representing Japan and coordinates youth service, continuity, and stakeholder alignment.
- Founder and Immediate Past President, KIST Key Club: Built the club to 40+ members and 5 executives, with service across health, caregiving, food insecurity, and youth service.
- Researcher, Lumiere Education: Cross-cultural communication and cognitive systems research; top-5% work scheduled for publication in NHSJS.
- Publications Specialist, Independent and collaborative research: Research writing on emoji semiotics, cognitive aging, and semiconductor supply-chain resilience.
- President, Japan Cancer Society, KIST: Organized a 10-kilometer walkathon with 500+ participants and led donation drives for cancer awareness and support.
- President, Recycling Initiative, KIST: Tripled membership to 60 and introduced Quality Inspector roles that improved oversight and recycling efficiency by 40%.

## Education
- Peterhouse, University of Cambridge: Official offer dated 2026-01-28; conditions: 42 points overall, 7,7,6 in three Higher Level subjects, and IELTS 8.0.
- K. International School Tokyo: Biology HL, English A HL, Chemistry HL, Mathematics AA SL, Japanese ab initio SL, Economics SL; predicted 45/45, SAT 1550, DET 160, UCAT 2410 Band 2.
- HarvardX MCB63X Principles of Biochemistry: Biochemistry coursework completed through HarvardX.
- Johns Hopkins CTY AP Biology: Advanced biology coursework completed through Johns Hopkins CTY.
- HKUST Dual Program in gifted education: Additional gifted-programme coursework referenced in application materials.

## Affiliations
- Kiwanis International: Service network and national leadership platform.
- Key Club International: Student service organization and leadership network.
- IEEE Hong Kong Section Education Chapter: Education chapter service and coordination.

## Activities
### Conference and committee service
- Organizing Committee Member, SEGA 2025: International Workshop on Smart Education in the Age of Generative AI.
- Technical Program Committee Member, IEEE SmartEdu 2026: Smart education conference program service.
- Technical Program Committee Member, IEEE Frontiers in Education: Program committee service across three editions.
- Technical Program Committee Member, IEEE EDUCON: Program committee service across two editions.
- Technical Program Committee Member, ICRCV: International Conference on Robotics and Computer Vision service.
- Technical Program Committee Member, ICCAE: International Conference on Computer and Automation Engineering.
- Technical Program Committee Member, IC&C / ICICC / ICCCC: International conference service across three editions.
- Technical Program Committee Member, ACAI: Algorithms, Computing and Artificial Intelligence program service.
- Technical Program Committee Member, MLNLP: Machine Learning and Natural Language Processing program service.
- Technical Program Committee Member, TALE: Teaching, Assessment, and Learning for Engineering program service.
- Technical Program Committee Member, ISCIPT: International Symposium on Computer and Information Processing Technology.

### Editorial and review work
- Session Chair: Conference session leadership across multiple international events.
- Journal Reviewer: Active and Passive Electronic Components; Analog Integrated Circuits and Signal Processing; Applied Sciences; Circuits, Systems and Signal Processing; Energies; IEEE Access; IEEE JSSC; IEEE JETCAS; IEEE Sensors Journal; IEEE TBioCAS; IEEE TCAS I/II; IEEE TIE; IEEE TPEL; IEEE TVLSI; IET Circuits, Devices & Systems; International Journal of Electronics; Journal of Circuits, Systems, and Computers; Microelectronics Journal; Microelectronics Reliability; Sustainability.

## Awards
### Academic recognition
- Peterhouse, University of Cambridge offer: BA (Hons) HSPS offer dated 2026-01-28; conditions: 42 points overall, 7,7,6 in three Higher Level subjects, and IELTS 8.0.
- Predicted IB 45/45: Official transcript shows six subjects predicted at 7, with TOK A, EE B, and core points 3.
- SAT 1550 · DET 160 · UCAT 2410 Band 2: Strong testing profile across language and admissions exams.
- KIST Honor Roll and Recognition: Honor Roll across 2023–2025, Certificate of Recognition 2023–2024, and International Research Olympiad Honor Roll 2025.
- Additional admissions offers: HKU, HKUST, Berkeley, UCLA, UCSD, Edinburgh, King’s, and UCL.
- Certificate of Distinction in the Annual Avogadro Exam: Annual chemistry competition recognition.
- Edexcel IGCSE Certificate of Excellence: Excellence recognition across IGCSE results.

### Research recognition
- Top 5% Lumiere proposal: Cross-cultural communication research on ambiguous emojis across Japanese and Chinese users.
- Emoji semiotics preprint: Preprint on Hofstede dimensions and generational effects in Japanese and Chinese digital communication.
- Cognitive aging review: Narrative review integrating six decades of the Seattle Longitudinal Study with contemporary advances.
- Semiconductor supply-chain concept paper: Concept paper on supply-chain resilience using big data analytics and quantum-enhanced blockchain.

### Competition recognition
- Harvard College Debate Union Regional Rounds Japan 2026: Certificate of Achievement for debate competition performance.
- Best Delegate, ASIJ MUN 2025: Model United Nations recognition.
- Diplomatic Commendation, JMMUN 2024: Model United Nations recognition.
- KPASS Junior Varsity Debate 2025: Debate competition recognition.
- High School Wrestling: Athletic competition recognition.

## Publications
- Hofstede’s Dimensions and Generational Effects on Ambiguous Emoji Semiotics: Cross-Cultural Analysis of Japanese and Chinese Digital Communication: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/emoji-semiotics/
- Unraveling Cognitive Aging: A Comprehensive Narrative Review Integrating Six Decades of the Seattle Longitudinal Study with Contemporary Advances: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/cognitive-aging/
- Semiconductor supply-chain resilience concept paper: https://thinkcollegelevel.com/blog/supply-chain-resilience/
